---
title: 'Curse Words...'
date: '2011-06-08T21:00:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '957074848425253299'
blogger_filename: '/2011/06/curse-words.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'study'
---

<span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: large;"><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/quicksearch/?quicksearch=blessing+and+cursing&amp;qs_version=NKJV">Search: "blessing and cursing"</a></span><br /><br /><ol class="keyword-result-list" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 16px;"><li style="margin-bottom: 10px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+30:19&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Deuteronomy 30:19</a></strong><br />I call heaven and earth as witnesses today against you, that I have set before you life and death,&nbsp;<b>blessing</b>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<b>cursing</b>; therefore choose life, that both you and your descendants may live;<br /><span class="keywordresultextras"><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+30:18-20&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Deuteronomy 30:18-20</a>&nbsp;(in Context)&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+30&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Deuteronomy 30</a>&nbsp;(Whole Chapter)</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Joshua+8:34&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Joshua 8:34</a></strong><br />And afterward he read all the words of the law, the&nbsp;<b>blessing</b>s and the&nbsp;<b>cursing</b>s, according to all that is written in the Book of the Law.<br /><span class="keywordresultextras"><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Joshua+8:33-35&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Joshua 8:33-35</a>&nbsp;(in Context)&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Joshua+8&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Joshua 8</a>&nbsp;(Whole Chapter)</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+109:17&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Psalm 109:17</a></strong><br />As he loved&nbsp;<b>cursing</b>, so let it come to him;As he did not delight in&nbsp;<b>blessing</b>, so let it be far from him.<br /><span class="keywordresultextras"><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+109:16-18&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Psalm 109:16-18</a>&nbsp;(in Context)&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+109&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Psalm 109</a>&nbsp;(Whole Chapter)</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=James+3:10&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">James 3:10</a></strong><br />Out of the same mouth proceed&nbsp;<b>blessing</b>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<b>cursing</b>. My brethren, these things ought not to be so.<br /><span class="keywordresultextras"><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=James+3:9-11&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">James 3:9-11</a>&nbsp;(in Context)&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=James+3&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">James 3</a>&nbsp;(Whole Chapter)</span></li></ol><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">A recent encounter has caused me to examine myself. From time to time my old nature comes out and I let loose what is known in&nbsp;English&nbsp;as a "Curse Word" It may be in a moment of physical pain, or mental/emotional frustration. I would like to say that I am redeemed enough to never let that happen, but that would be lie. From time to time it does, then I repent. Nevertheless, I desire to grow into the likeness of God. Therefore I started praying about this today.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">What is a curse word. If you just say "Curse" it brings to the western mind an image of a witch casting a spell, or a cursed place. It brings to mind those who are afraid to break a mirror because they don't want 7 years bad luck. It's unfortunate to say that you find such nonsense even within the walls of of the Church. But in light of vocabulary and descriptives, what is a curse word?</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">To understand any issue, one must find it's root. The root for this, as with so many things, goes right back to Genesis Chapter 1:28. The beginning of man. God says:&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"></span></span><br /><div class="heading passage-class-0" style="color: #5c1101; margin-top: 10px;"><h3 style="font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=genesis%201:27-30&amp;version=NKJV">Genesis 1:27-30</a></span></span></h3><div class="txt-sm" style="font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;">New King James Version (NKJV)</span></span></div></div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-27" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">27</sup>&nbsp;So God created man in His&nbsp;<i>own</i>&nbsp;image; in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">28</sup>&nbsp;Then <b>God blessed them</b>, and God said to them,<b> “Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it; have dominion</b> over the fish of the sea, over the birds of the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth.”<br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">29</sup>&nbsp;And God said, “See, I have given you every herb&nbsp;<i>that</i>&nbsp;yields seed which&nbsp;<i>is</i>&nbsp;on the face of all the earth, and every tree whose fruit yields seed; to you it shall be for food.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-30" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">30</sup>&nbsp;Also, to every beast of the earth, to every bird of the air, and to everything that creeps on the earth, in which&nbsp;<i>there is</i>&nbsp;life,&nbsp;<i>I have given</i>&nbsp;every green herb for food”; and it was so.</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">The Blessing was mans initiation onto planet earth. It was, is, and forever will be the command of God to all men and women. We are created in His image. We are, when functioning correctly, tied into and flowing in HIS nature, which is a creative nature, it is a blessing nature, it is a fruitful nature.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">THE Blessing on Adam was given to him to have a domain, and cause that domain to&nbsp;flourish&nbsp;with the Life force of God. When Adam disconnected from God and connected to Lucifer he did NOT create the curse. Neither Satan nor Adam could create. God is the creator. Man creates only through his connection to the creator. When disconnected from God Adam disconnected from the flowing force and power of life. Life is not a state of being, or an event, it is an ongoing creative force. Death is not the opposite of life, it is the lack of it. The force of death is nothing more than the force which cuts off the force of life.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">THE Curse, therefore, is the force and power of death which is that which cuts one off from life. It is not as powerful as Life. For only LIFE is a power unto itself. Rather, it is a force one chooses to enter into and it's affect is to remove one from the flow and force of life. It is as though one places a kink in the hose and the life force stops flowing or slows. The reason Adam died physically was because life stopped functioning, but there was so much life in him that it took him over 900 years to die physically after he died spiritually. However the hose was turned off the VERY moment he disconnected from the LIFE of God.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">Back to Curse Words. ALL forces in life are connected to the Blessing or the Curse.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">Even a word as seemingly nuetral as "THE" can connect one to the blessing or curse depending on the heart from which is was spoken.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">:)</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">"The Bird!" Spoken in hate connects one to the curse and death.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">&gt;:(</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">"The Bird!" Spoken in adoration of the creation and glory of God's work connects one to the blessing.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">So then Curse Words, are words which connect us to the curse. To say that you are merely expressing yourself and that it's your right is true and fine. But where did you get the right to express yourself. Where did that right come from? It came from Genesis 1:28, in fact, more specifically, it came from Deuteronomy 30:19.</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+30:19&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Deuteronomy 30:19</a></strong><br />I call heaven and earth as witnesses today against you, that I have set before you life and death,&nbsp;<b>blessing</b>&nbsp;and<b>cursing</b>; therefore choose life, that both you and your descendants may live;</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">God did in fact let you choose which words you would connect your life to. But you will also be reaping the result of those choices and words.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+109:17&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #b2462d; text-decoration: underline;">Psalm 109:17</a></strong><br />As he loved&nbsp;<b>cursing</b>, so let it come to him;As he did not delight in&nbsp;<b>blessing</b>, so let it be far from him.</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><br /></span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=James+3:10&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #b2462d; text-decoration: underline;">James 3:10</a></strong><br />Out of the same mouth proceed&nbsp;<b>blessing</b>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<b>cursing</b>. My brethren, these things ought not to be so.</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><br /></span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;">So as I examine my life I must begin to make very slow, deliberate choices. What words will I use?&nbsp;</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><br /></span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+30:14&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #b2462d; text-decoration: underline;">Deuteronomy 30:14</a></strong><br />But the word is very near you, in your&nbsp;<b>mouth</b>&nbsp;and in your<b>heart</b>, that you may do it.</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><br /></span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+15:18&amp;version=50" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Matthew 15:18</a></strong><br />But those things which proceed out of the&nbsp;<b>mouth</b>&nbsp;come from the&nbsp;<b>heart</b>, and they defile a man.</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><br /></span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+6:45&amp;version=50" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;">Luke 6:45</a></strong><br />A good man out of the good treasure of his&nbsp;<b>heart</b>&nbsp;brings forth good; and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his&nbsp;<b>heart</b>&nbsp;brings forth evil. For out of the abundance of the<b>heart</b>&nbsp;his&nbsp;<b>mouth</b>&nbsp;speaks.</span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-size: 16px;"><br /></span></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"></span><br /><div class="heading passage-class-0" style="color: #5c1101; font-size: 16px; margin-top: 10px;"><h3 style="font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">Romans 10:8-10</span></h3><div class="txt-sm" style="font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">New King James Version (NKJV)</span></div></div><div style="font-size: 16px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28193" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">8</sup>&nbsp;But what does it say?&nbsp;<i>“The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart”</i><sup class="footnote" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: 0.5em; vertical-align: text-top;" value="[&lt;a href=&quot;#fen-NKJV-28193a&quot; title=&quot;See footnote a&quot;&gt;a&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+10:8-10&amp;version=NKJV#fen-NKJV-28193a" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;" title="See footnote a">a</a>]</sup>(that is, the word of faith which we preach):<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28194" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">9</sup>&nbsp;that if you confess with your mouth the Lord Jesus and believe in your heart that God has raised Him from the dead, you will be saved.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28195" style="font-size: 0.65em; font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">10</sup>&nbsp;For with the heart one believes unto righteousness, and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.</span></div><div style="font-size: 16px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="font-size: 16px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">Your heart spoken through your mouth will cause you to&nbsp;receive.<br /><br /><br />I have the right to use any word which I choose. I can even use curse words and still go to heaven. But if I make that choice not much of Heaven will function in my life while I'm waiting to go. I must begin to choose my words with more care and crafting. I am in fact crafting my life with them.<br /><br />This also means I should be less inclined to speak whatever comes to my mind to people. Even if it seems like a harmless&nbsp;piece&nbsp;of information given to a cashier at a store, did I need to say that? Was it connected to the blessing of the curse. Even "Have a nice day!" in the wrong heart will create the curse and not the blessing.... choose... life...<br /><br />Darrell G. Wolfe<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div>
